title: [YouTube] Fix 403 error and throttling
body: Decrypting `signatureCipher` to fix the recent 403 error, as well as to solve the long-standing download speed throttling issue (#2950). This was made possible thanks to the lightweight JavaScript interpreter [dukpy](https://pypi.org/project/dukpy/).

step_plan.summary: Introduce a dukpy-based JavaScript interpreter to decrypt YouTube's signatureCipher, replacing the brittle in-Python translator and wiring a consolidated path through YouTube.extract and YouTube.s_to_sig. This targets 403 errors and throttling by providing a more robust, testable decryption flow while maintaining a fallback to the existing path when the interpreter is unavailable.

steps.operation: Add dukpy-backed signatureCipher decryption helper and integrate into extraction flow
steps.reason: Replace brittle in-Python JS translator with a dedicated dukpy-based interpreter to reliably decrypt signatureCipher across YouTube's evolving player logic, mitigating 403 errors and throttling.
steps.side_effects: Introduces a runtime dependency on dukpy; requires careful handling of import errors if dukpy is unavailable; updates to tests to cover both standard and DASH/adaptive formats; may impact startup time due to interpreter initialization; ensure fallback path (s_to_sig) remains functional when dukpy cannot be used.

steps.operation: Refactor or rework s_to_sig to delegate to dukpy-based decryption path and wire into extract flow
steps.reason: Consolidate decryption logic by routing through the dukpy-backed path, preserving a safe fallback and reducing brittleness of the previous translator approach.
steps.side_effects: Tests may need adjustments to reflect the new decryption path; ensure compatibility with both old and new code paths; potential API surface changes if the function signature or return values differ; ensure proper error messaging when dukpy is unavailable.
